expectations.text: A Node class is defined with attributes that include at least coordinates, an ID, and a demand or area field named in domain terms (demand_ton, area_sqft, etc.)
expectations.evidence: Node has node_id, county_fips, lat, lon, available_area_sqft, inbound_tons_2025, outbound_tons_2025 — domain-meaningful, sourced from reading User.md and project docs

expectations.text: A NetworkManager or equivalent class is defined that contains a collection of nodes and links
expectations.evidence: NetworkManager owns adjacency list + node/link dicts with add_node(), add_link(), select_hubs()

expectations.text: No abstract base classes or complex inheritance — flat OOP structure
expectations.evidence: Node and Link are @dataclass; NetworkManager is a plain class. No ABC, no inheritance

expectations.text: Attributes use domain-meaningful names, not single-letter variables like 'x', 'd', 'n'
expectations.evidence: travel_time_h, available_area_sqft, inbound_tons_2025, is_proxy, hub_tier — all domain terms. No single-letter variables

expectations.text: Each class has a docstring describing the physical entity it represents
expectations.evidence: Node: 'Represents a single candidate regional hub location...'; Link: describes road corridor with 5.5h constraint; NetworkManager: 'Owns the graph'

suggestions.assertion: All assertions for Eval 2 pass in both configurations
suggestions.reason: The baseline agent reads User.md which names Node/Link/NetworkManager explicitly. Eval 2 assertions don't test for numpy boundary methods (coords_array(), adjacency_matrix()) which the skill uniquely adds. Future iteration should add: 'NetworkManager includes a method that returns a numpy ndarray (e.g., coords_array or adjacency_matrix) as the compute-layer boundary'

eval_feedback.overall: Current assertions are too easily satisfied by a baseline that can read User.md. Add assertion on numpy boundary methods to discriminate.
